[[BLOCK:p_3]]
PSYM 5000 Course Syllabus

[[BLOCK:p_4]]
Spring 2026

[[BLOCK:p_6]]
Course Title and Number: PSYM 5000

[[BLOCK:p_7]]
Course Title: Introduction to Psychology (心理学导论)  
Semester: Spring 2026

[[BLOCK:p_8]]
Course Format: Asynchronous online course (异步，线上课程)

[[BLOCK:p_10]]
Name: Hui Zhang

[[BLOCK:p_11]]
Location: Online Instruction

[[BLOCK:p_13]]
This course is taught by Professor Hui Zhang, who brings over 20 years of extensive experience in public education lectures in psychology, psychological counseling services, and professional skills training, with a particular focus on individual and group counseling. Professor Zhang earned a Bachelor’s degree in Preventive Medicine, a Master’s degree in Management Psychology, and a Ph.D. in Psychiatry and Mental Health from Capital Medical University (首都医科大学), establishing a strong interdisciplinary foundation bridging medicine and psychology.

Professor Zhang has long taught core undergraduate and graduate courses, including Medical Psychology and Mental Health, and previously led the development of a nationally recognized high-quality open online course as the principal instructor. Her teaching achievements have been widely recognized, including receiving an Excellence Award in the National Medical Humanities Course Teaching Competition for Higher Medical Institutions. As either principal investigator or key collaborator, she has led or participated in multiple national- and provincial-level research projects. She has published numerous articles in leading academic journals, many indexed in the Science Citation Index (SCI) (SCI) and the Social Sciences Citation Index (SSCI). She has also served as chief editor or contributing editor for several scholarly works and nationally planned textbooks, including Emotional Intelligence Development and Stress Management and Practical Medical Psychology.

[[BLOCK:p_15]]
Course Description:

[[BLOCK:p_16]]
This course serves as a reintroduction to psychology and is designed for graduate students who are new to the field or returning to it after prior study. It covers the fundamental processes of behavior, cognition, and emotion, as well as their applications in everyday life and specialized contexts.
